items.title: Arkansas SNAP waiver will block benefits from buying soda and candy starting July 1
items.summary: USDA approved Arkansas’ request for a two-year SNAP demonstration that takes effect July 1, 2026. The waiver excludes soda (including low- and no-calorie soda), fruit/vegetable drinks with less than 50% juice, other unhealthy drinks, and candy from SNAP-eligible purchases.

items.title: CMS released the March 2026 Medicaid/CHIP eligibility operations snapshot
items.summary: CMS posted its Medicaid and CHIP Eligibility Operations and Enrollment Snapshot covering March 2026 on June 26, 2026. The report provides state and national operational metrics for eligibility processing and enrollment operations (the webpage links to the full snapshot and related materials).

items.title: SSA confirms July 2026 payment dates for SSI and Social Security benefits
items.summary: SSA’s 2026–2027 payment schedule lists SSI payments for July 2026 on July 1, 2026. Social Security benefit payments for July 2026 are scheduled for July 8 (birthdays 1–10), July 15 (11–20), and July 22 (21–31).

items.title: IRS: maximum Additional Child Tax Credit is $1,700 per child for 2026
items.summary: The IRS’s 2026 Form 1040-ES (NR) instructions state that for 2026, the maximum Additional Child Tax Credit amount is $1,700 for each qualifying child. The same document lists 2026 estimated-tax due dates of April 15, June 15, September 15, 2026, and January 15, 2027.

items.title: HUD and VA announce $33 million for 2,532 new HUD-VASH vouchers for homeless veterans
items.summary: HUD and VA announced $33 million to support 2,532 new HUD-VASH vouchers across 265 Public Housing Authorities in 44 states. HUD also announced $10 million in administrative fees across 289 PHAs in 46 states to help lease HUD-VASH vouchers and improve program delivery.

items.title: HUD announces 14 FHA Single Family policy changes, including appraisal field-review savings
items.summary: HUD announced 14 policy changes to its FHA Single Family program on June 23, 2026. HUD said streamlining appraisal field-review requirements could cut an average $425 per field review and is expected to save about $3.3 million annually.

items.title: USDA outlines WIC food package changes, including higher fruit/vegetable cash-value benefits
items.summary: USDA’s WIC food packages guidance lists base monthly cash-value benefit amounts of $24 for children, $43 for pregnant/postpartum participants, and $47 for breastfeeding participants (adjusted annually for inflation). The same guidance lists FY 2024 inflation-adjusted levels at implementation as $26 (children), $47 (pregnant/postpartum), and $52 (breastfeeding).

items.title: 2026–27 FAFSA cycle: applications accepted through June 30, 2027 (online and paper)
items.summary: The Federal Student Aid Handbook’s 2026–27 application-process guidance states the online FAFSA for 2026–27 was available to all applicants by October 1, 2025. It also states that 2026–27 FAFSA applications will be accepted through June 30, 2027 (online submissions must be received by that date, and paper forms must be received by that date).
items.source: FSA Partner Connect (U.S. Department of Education)
